name: Chocolate Mint Pinwheels
recipeInstructions:
1 Melt half a cup of chocolate mint chips over hot (not boiling) water until smooth. Cool to room temperature and set aside.
2 In a large bowl, combine butter and sugar; beat until creamy. Add egg and vanilla extract, blending well.
3 Gradually beat in flour and salt. Place one cup of dough in a small bowl. Add melted chips; blend thoroughly.
4 Shape into a ball and flatten (mixture will appear curdled). Cover with plastic wrap.
5 Shape remaining dough into a ball and flatten. Cover with plastic wrap. Chill until firm, about 1 1/2 hours.
6 Preheat oven to 375°F. Between sheets of waxed paper, roll each ball of dough into a 13 x 9-inch rectangle.
7 Remove top layers of waxed paper and invert the chocolate dough onto the plain dough.
8 Peel off waxed paper. Starting from the long side, roll up into a jelly roll style.
9 Cut into 1/4-inch slices; place on ungreased cookie sheets.
10 Bake at 375°F for 7 to 8 minutes. Cool completely on wire racks.
11 Melt remaining chips over hot (not boiling) water until smooth. Spread flat side of each cookie with a slightly rounded teaspoonful melted chocolate. Chill until set.
